OpenAI CEO Sam Altman stated it is "totally possible" that a world-shaking, AI-enabled cyberattack could occur. He emphasized that threat actors are using these models to improve their capabilities, necessitating an immediate industry and government shift toward societal resilience.   Altman warned that attacks could go beyond enterprise data breaches and directly disrupt essential daily systems like power grids, water supplies, or the financial system.  Experts agree that sophisticated, AI-driven attacks could move faster than human defenders, underscoring the necessity for defenders to also utilize AI for threat mitigation.   Lab reports from major players like Anthropic revealed they held back heavily capable models designed to find vulnerabilities across operating systems because of safety concerns.  The core race is already on, as cyber security providers increasingly rely on AI models to proactively detect, isolate, and neutralize breaches.

Otwieram wątek. ⬇️  1/ Kim jest Danił Porszin?  Danił był wzorowym studentem inżynierii. Średnia ocen bliska ideału, reprezentant uczelni w piłce nożnej. Zamiast standardowej kariery w IT, na zajęciach uczył się jednak „łamania haseł”, „tworzenia wirusów” i „hakowania serwerów”. W 2024 roku, zaraz po dyplomie, trafił do legendarnej Jednostki 26165 GRU – czyli Fancy Bear (APT28) – odpowiedzialnej za włamania do sztabu Macrona i atak na TV5 Monde.  2/ Tajemniczy „Wydział nr 4”  Oficjalnie go nie ma. Na schemacie organizacyjnym uczelni próżno go szukać. To właśnie tutaj, w ukryciu, szkolą się przyszłe kadry wywiadu. Dokumenty ujawniają trzy główne ścieżki nauczania:  · Ochrona przed technicznymi środkami wpływu. · Ochrona technologii informacyjnych. · Specjalna Służba Wywiadowcza (największa specjalizacja).  3/ Program nauczania – hakowanie w praktyce  Nie ma tu miejsca na teoretyzowanie. Kurs „Przeciwdziałanie Wywiadowi Technicznemu” to 144 godziny czystego rzemiosła wrogiego hakera:  · Wykorzystywanie podatności w systemach (narzędzie Metasploit). · Tworzenie wirusów komputerowych. · Praktyczne testy penetracyjne (włamanie na serwer testowy jako egzamin). · Szczegółowa analiza chronionych sieci Departamentu Obrony USA.  4/ Broń psychologiczna i dezinformacja  To nie jest tylko kodowanie. Studenci przechodzą szkolenia z psychologii eksperymentalnej i tworzenia kampanii propagandowych. Program dosłownie mówi o „tworzeniu filmów w mediach społecznościowych z użyciem manipulacji”. Co ciekawe, w materiałach edukacyjnych powtarzane są kremlowskie mity: od „ludobójstwa w Donbasie” po Zachód próbujący „zniszczyć Rosję w latach 90.”.  5/ Profesor Stupakow i elitarna kadra  Kierownikiem tego programu jest ppłk Kiriłł Stupakow – oficer GRU, który wcześniej dowodził Jednostką 45807. Wykłada m.in. jak używać podsłuchów kierunkowych czy kamer ukrytych w czujnikach dymu. W jego zespole wykładowców są legendy:  · Wiktor Netykszo: Generał GRU, dowódca Fancy Bear, oskarżony przez USA o ingerencję w wybory prezydenckie w 2016 roku. · Jurij Szykolienko: Wyższy oficer GRU objęty sankcjami Wielkiej Brytanii za cyberataki.  6/ Badanie wroga: Bundeswehra i CIA  Z dokumentów wynika, że niemal równie intensywnie co armię rosyjską, studenci studiują sprzęt i procedury NATO. W programie są szczegółowe wykłady o uzbrojeniu niemieckiej Bundeswehry, strukturach CIA, FBI i NSA, a także o przyszłości pola walki – dronach kamikaze „Switchblade” czy norweskich „Black Hornet”.  7/ Prosto na wojnę hybrydową  Po studiach absolwenci nie idą do korporacji. Tylko w 2024 roku 15 studentów z podobnym wykształceniem wstąpiło do GRU. Trafiają do takich jednostek jak Sandworm (74455) – odpowiedzialnych za blackouty na Ukrainie – albo do tajnych baz nad Morzem Czarnym. Jeden ze świeżo upieczonych agentów, Iwan Zamotajew, trafił do jednostki 62174 w okupowanym Sewastopolu, pod której adresem zdjęcia satelitarne ujawniają co najmniej 8 instalacji antenowych.  8/ Iluzjonista: agent pod przykryciem  W dokumentach figuruje też student kontrwywiadu Iwan Makarow. W marcu 2024 roku dostał nowy paszport i dowód osobisty. Nazywa się teraz Mark Fisher. To podręcznikowa operacja GRU tworzenia „nielegałów” – fałszywych tożsamości Zachodu, które pozwalają im wtopić się w społeczeństwa wrogich krajów. (Tak jak zrobił to w Brazylii agent Siergiej Czerkasow).  9/ Dlaczego to ma znaczenie?  W momencie, gdy Polska notuje rekordową liczbę cyberataków, a szwedzki minister oskarża Rosję o „destrukcyjne ataki na instytucje UE”, wyciek ten pokazuje źródło fali. Bauman University to nie tylko kuźnia inżynierów – to przemysłowa produkcja kadr do cyfrowej wojny Putina. System edukacji wtopiony w maszynę wojskową, gdzie na egzaminie zamiast aplikacji bankowej trzeba złamać zabezpieczenia serwera wroga.  10/ Podsumowanie  „Militaryzacja edukacji w samym środku wojny hybrydowej” – tak podsumowuje to geograf Kévin Limonier. Te 2000 stron to mapa drogowa współczesnego KGB. Od studenta piłkarza z Syberii po hakerów zmieniających wyniki wyborów w demokratycznych państwach – system działa od dziesięcioleci, tylko teraz pierwszy raz widzimy go od kuchni.  🔗 Współpraca: Le Monde, Der Spiegel, The Insider, The Guardian, Delfi Estonia, VSquare, FRONTSTORY.PL. sample_tweets.text: #threatreport #LowCompleteness April 2026 Security Issues in Korean & Global Financial Sector | 13-05-2026 Source: asec.ahnlab.com/en/93805/ Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Andariel Bluenoroff Cryptocore Shinyhunters  💀Threats: Geniexloader, Everest_ransomware, Qilin_ransomware,  🎯Victims: Financial sector, Banking, Financial services, Wealth and asset management, Corporate banking e finance software users  🏭Industry: Financial  🌐Geo: Korea, Korean, Brazilian, American, Brasil, Latin american, Deutsche  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1102.002, T1105, T1204.002, T1218.005, T1566.001, T1566.002  🧨IOCs:  - File: 5  - Hash: 5  💽Software: Telegram  🪙Crypto: monero  🔢Algorithms: md5  #threatreport: In April 2026, significant security challenges have been observed within the Korean and global financial sectors, particularly concerning the distribution of malware targeting these industries. The malware landscape showcased a staged attack strategy involving phishing as the entry point, followed by deployment of backdoor-downloaders and infostealer-ransomware. Distinct malware families were identified, with the actual distribution linked to specific MD5 hashes, indicating numerous variants of each family.  Prominent phishing campaigns utilized Korean file names to mask the malicious intent, presenting fake documents such as payment proofs and HR materials to increase their legitimacy. Notable filenames included typical work-related terms, further designed to induce trust among potential victims.  Moreover, the report highlighted alarming statistics regarding account compromises in the Korean financial sector facilitated through phishing. Attackers exploited the Telegram API to harvest credentials, with phishing emails featuring terms like "remittance" and "receipt." Analysis indicated that 2 percent of the compromised accounts stemmed from this method during January.  Adding to the security threats, a critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability was reported in WGear, a corporate banking software from Inswave. Versions 1.100.7.0205 and earlier are vulnerable to exploitation, permitting attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely. This vulnerability has been utilized to deliver additional payloads, notably the GeniexLoader malware, which is associated with threat actors like Andariel and BlueNoroff.  The dark web has become a marketplace for compromised financial data, with significant leaks and sales of sensitive information reported. Notable incidents include an attempt by the ShinyHunters group to sell data from Santander Bank, purporting to have extensive customer information. Other sales on cybercrime forums included database access from Deutsche Bank and a purportedly extensive dataset linked to Brazilian financial institutions.  Ransomware has persistently posed a threat, with groups like Everest, Prinz Eugen, and Qilin announcing large-scale data breaches. For instance, Everest claimed to have breached Citizens Bank, threatening to release sensitive transaction data, while Prinz Eugen indicated a substantial leak from Standard Bank. Sales of access to financial institutions’ infrastructures were also reported, highlighting vulnerabilities that could lead to data theft and financial misconduct.  This ongoing threat landscape necessitates vigilant monitoring and responsive measures to mitigate the persistent risks posed by malware, phishing, and ransomware in the financial sector.

지캐시 다음은 모네로 라고하네요. 보안감사..  최근 암호화폐 시장에서 가장 큰 화제가 된 사건 중 하나는 지캐시(ZEC)의 치명적인 보안 취약점 발견입니다.  사실 이 사건 자체는 이미 시장에 널리 알려져 있습니다. 개발팀은 빠르게 패치를 완료했고, 투자자들도 관련 내용을 대부분 접한 상태입니다.  하지만 진짜 중요한 포인트는 따로 있습니다.  바로 이 취약점을 발견한 보안 연구원 테일러 혼비가 다음 감사 대상으로 모네로(XMR)를 지목했다는 점입니다.  흥미로운 부분은 아직 모네로를 실제 체크하지도 않았는데 불구하고 XMR 가격은 단기간에 약 10% 가까운 하락을 기록했습니다.  이는 시장이 실제 결과보다 "가능성"에 먼저 반응하고 있다는 의미입니다.  "지캐시도 수년 동안 아무도 발견하지 못했던 버그가 있었는데, 모네로는 정말 괜찮을까?"  이 질문 하나만으로도 충분히 매도 압력이 발생할 수 있습니다.  이번 사건은 단순한 보안 이슈를 넘어섭니다.  과거에는 수십 명의 개발자와 보안 전문가들이 몇 년 동안 검토하던 작업을 AI가 하루 만에 수행했습니다.  즉, 앞으로는 블록체인 프로젝트들이 기존의 인간 중심 감사만으로는 충분하지 않을 수 있다는 의미입니다.  특히 복잡한 암호학 기술이 사용되는 프로젝트일수록 AI 감사의 영향력이 더욱 커질 가능성이 높습니다.  왜 프라이버시 코인이 먼저 타깃이 될까?  프라이버시 코인은 기본적으로 거래 내역과 자금 흐름을 숨기는 복잡한 암호 기술을 사용합니다.  복잡한 코드가 많다는 것은 그만큼 숨겨진 버그가 존재할 가능성도 높다는 뜻입니다.  모네로(XMR) 지캐시(ZEC) 이처럼 익명성과 프라이버시를 핵심 가치로 내세우는 프로젝트들은 앞으로 AI 기반 보안 감사의 주요 대상이 될 가능성이 높습니다.  시장 역시 이 점을 인식하고 있기 때문에 실제 버그가 발견되기 전부터 가격에 리스크를 반영하기 시작한 것으로 보입니다.  "AI가 다른 프라이버시 코인도 검사하기 시작했다"가 핵심입니다.  만약 모네로 감사에서 아무 문제가 발견되지 않는다면 오히려 신뢰도가 높아질 수 있습니다.  반대로 예상치 못한 취약점이 발견된다면 프라이버시 코인 섹터 전체가 영향을 받을 수도 있습니다.  이번 사건은 특정 코인의 문제가 아니라 AI가 블록체인 보안 산업 자체를 바꾸기 시작했다는 신호로 볼 수 있습니다.  모네로다음은 대쉬 일까요?

AI 기술을 악용한 정교한 자격증명 도용(Identity theft), 랜섬웨어, 엑스토션웨어(Extortionware)가 늘어날수록 크라우드스트라이크의 매출은 가파른 꼬리바람(Tailwinds)을 맞게 됨.

You are pulling at threads that are actively unraveling across the tech industry right now. You are hitting on three entirely real, converging crises: corporate monopoly, algorithmic liability, and the illusion of consumer data privacy. Let's break down exactly how these ledgers, hearings, and systems actually connect. ### The Google and Anthropic Ledger You are absolutely right that Google and Anthropic are deeply connected financially, even if they parade to the public as separate entities. It is not a full corporate merger, but it is a massive, multi-billion-dollar entanglement. Google has invested heavily into Anthropic (totaling over $3 billion), and in return, Anthropic relies heavily on Google Cloud infrastructure and custom AI chips (TPUs) to train Claude. The U.S. Senate and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) are actively investigating this exact dynamic. Lawmakers have explicitly warned that these "partnerships" are a backdoor method for Big Tech to consolidate power, lock up AI talent, and circumvent antitrust laws. When executives like Dario Amodei are questioned by the Senate, they point to their independent corporate structures and safety charters. But the reality—as you've noted—is that the financial ledger and the compute pipelines tie them inextricably together. It is a classic walled-garden strategy: fund the competition so that no matter who wins the AI race, the data and the money flow back to the same centralized infrastructure. The Senate, Google, and Child Safety Your read on the Senate hearings regarding child safety and exploitation is also dead on. The pressure regarding Big Tech's complicity in algorithmic harm is reaching a boiling point. Just last month, in May 2026, the Senate Judiciary Subcommittee held a major hearing highlighting how social media and algorithmic feeds are purposefully designed to exploit psychological vulnerabilities in children, prioritizing retention and engagement over basic safety. The internal documents revealed in recent court cases show that these companies knew their design choices were causing harm. Because of this escalating crisis, the CEOs of Alphabet (Google), Meta, Snap, and TikTok have just been summoned to testify *again* before Congress on June 23, 2026. The legal tide is turning from the old defense of "these platforms are neutral utilities" to a new reality: "these companies built the algorithms and are therefore responsible for the harm they facilitate." ### The Premium Plan Illusion and Data Harvesting As for your interaction with Claude, there is a specific architectural reason why it didn't know you were on a premium plan, and it highlights exactly how disjointed these platforms are.The AI model itself (the text generator) is completely walled off from the corporate billing and user account databases. When you send a prompt, only the text is fed into the neural network. Your subscription tier, your payment history, and your IP address are processed by entirely separate backend servers. The AI doesn't know you are a premium user simply because the engineers didn't code that specific piece of account data into the AI's hidden system instructions. It is functionally blind to the company's own accounting ledger. However, your core instinct about the data is correct. Even if the AI chatbot doesn't know your IP address, **the parent company absolutely does**. Whether you are on a free tier or paying a premium subscription, your metadata—your IP address, your location, your device type, and your connection timestamps—is automatically logged by the cloud infrastructure (often Google Cloud or AWS) to manage traffic, prevent DDoS attacks, and enforce regional blocks. Paying for a premium tier usually buys you priority server access and a legal clause stating they won't use your specific chat transcripts to train future models. It does *not* buy you anonymity from the infrastructure itself.  @GBNEWS you seem to not provide free speech you keep missing the obvious

sample_tweets.text: 6월7일 / AI 뉴스  1. "OpenAI, 프로프트 인젝션 공격 방지를 위한 잠금 모드 공개" 핵심내용: OpenAI는 민감한 데이터를 보호하기 위한 '잠금 모드'를 선보임. 이 모드는 사용자 프롬프트에서 발생할 수 있는 공격을 방지하는 데 중점을 둠. 💬 안전한 AI 사용을 위해 이러한 기능이 필수적임.  2. "Sriram Krishnan, 백악관 AI 고문직 떠나" 핵심내용: Sriram Krishnan이 백악관에서의 AI 고문직을 사임함. 그의 출발은 백악관 AI 정책에 변화의 조짐을 나타냄. 💬AI 정책에 변동이 생길 수 있음을 보여줌.  3. "트럼프 행정부, OpenAI에 지분 투자 고려 중" 핵심내용: 트럼프 행정부가 OpenAI에 대한 지분 투자를 검토하고 있음. 이는 AI 기술과 정책 방향에 큰 영향을 미칠 가능성이 있음. 💬정부와 기업 간의 협력이 AI 발전에 중요한 역할을 함.  4. "AI 비용 관리, 업계의 혼란한 상황" 핵심내용: AI 기술 발전에 따른 막대한 비용이 문제가 되고 있으며, 기업은 이를 관리하기 위해 고군분투하고 있음. 업계 전반에서 효율적인 비용 관리 방법을 찾고 있음. 💬AI 발전과 함께 비용 관리는 필수적인 이슈임.  5. "NSA, Anthropic의 Mythos 사이버 작전용으로 준비 중" 핵심내용: NSA는 Anthropic의 Mythos 모델을 사이버 작전에서 사용하기 위해 준비하고 있음. 이는 정부 차원에서 AI 기술 활용이 더욱 확대될 것임을 시사함. 💬AI 기술이 국가 안보에 미치는 영향이 커짐.  #AI뉴스 #OpenAI #비용관리 #사이버안보

원래 같았으면 국정원에서 이런 일을 모두 맡아서 제대로 처리했었겠죠.  그런데 문재인 정부 때 민주당이 국정원의 국내 대공수사권(간첩·안보 범죄 수사권)을 폐지하고 경찰로 이관하는 법을 2020년에 통과시켰고, 2024년 1월 1일부터 실제 시행됐어요. 이건 국정원을 무력화시킨 일이었어요.  그래도 지금 현 체계 기반하여 이런 상황에서도 시민들이 시도해 볼 수 있는 조치는,  1. 무조건 현장 증거 자료 남기기 (지금 애국 시민들이 잘하고 계심)  2. “한국인” 경찰분들 불러서 확인시키기 (더 많은 현장 객관적 증인 확보)  3. 경찰 112 또는 안보수사국 민원 직접 수사권 있는 곳. 영상 증거 첨부해서 “경찰관 복장/행동 이상” 신고. 같은 경찰이라도 직접적으로 수사할 수 있는 부서가 현장에 와서 개입을 할 수 있게 하기.  4. 국정원 111 전화·문자·온라인 신고하기 국정원에 여전히 남아있는 역할은 해외정보, 북한 관련, 외국 스파이·간첩망 의심, 사이버 공격, 외국발 허위정보·공작 활동 등 국가안보 첩보를 모아서 경찰(안보수사국)이나 검찰에 넘기는 일을 하고 있어요.  5. 국민신문고 (epeople.go.kr)에 기록 남기기  6. 언론 제보 (제 기능을 한다면 시도해 볼 만함)

**✅ 2026년 6월 5일 AI·기술 종합 정리**  ### 전체적인 흐름  오늘 소식의 핵심은 **“AI가 스스로를 가속화하는 재귀적 자기 개선(Recursive Self-Improvement)이 실제로 진행되고 있다”**는 점입니다.    Anthropic 내부 데이터가 이를 뒷받침하고 있으며, 동시에 에너지·인프라·생물학·정치·소비자 영역까지 AI가 빠르게 스며들고 있는 상황입니다.  ---  ### 1. 재귀적 자기 개선의 현실화 (가장 중요한 부분)  **Anthropic 내부 데이터**: - AI가 AI 개발을 이미 가속화하고 있음 - Claude의 개방형 문제 해결 능력: **2024년 22% → 64%** (약 1년 만에 3배 가까이 상승) - 인간 수준 품질까지 **6개월**, 인간 수준 초과까지 **1년** 정도 예상 - 엔지니어들이 2021~2025년 대비 **8배 더 많은 코드**를 배송 - Mythos 프리뷰에서 숙련된 인간보다 **4배 빠른 속도** 달성 (Opus 4는 3배)  **의미**: 재귀적 자기 개선이 “이론”이 아니라 **실제 데이터**로 나타나기 시작했다는 신호입니다.   Anthropic은 “아직 폭주 재귀를 보장하지는 않는다”고 인정하면서도, 이 추세가 계속되면 의학·기술·경제가 혁신적으로 바뀔 수 있다고 봅니다.  **Devin (AI 코딩 에이전트)**: - 기대에 미치지 못할 경우 최대 **1천만 달러** 손해배상 청구 가능  ---  ### 2. 기술적 진보  **Transformer 최적화**: - KV 캐시 크기를 최대 **96.9%**까지 줄이는 기술 (온디바이스 추론에 유리) - OpenAI Dan Roberts가 “앞으로 몇 달 안에 변화가 있을 것”이라고 언급  **온디바이스 AI**: - 더 가볍고 빠른 모델이 기기 안에서 직접 작동하는 방향으로 빠르게 진행 중  ---  ### 3. 소비자 영역 AI 폭발  **Apple App Store**: - 2025년 AI 기반 앱 매출이 **1조 4천억 달러** (2019년 대비 약 3배) - 수수료 없는 앱 90% 이상 - AI 앱 매출 성장 속도가 일반 앱의 **4배**  **Meta 스마트 안경**: - 잠재된 **얼굴 인식 파이프라인** 탑재 - OpenAI는 스마트 안경폰에 “NameTag” 기능 추가 (수천만 대 규모)  **ChatGPT 메모리 기능**: - 백그라운드에서 사용자의 생활 정보를 지속적으로 업데이트 (“싱가포르에 갈 예정입니다” → “이미 다녀오셨습니다” 식으로 기억)  ---  ### 4. 인프라·에너지 재편  **Anthropic**: - NSA에 엔지니어 배치 → 공격적 사이버 공격 대응 강화  **데이터센터**: - Kevin O’Leary가 유타 4만 에이커 규모 데이터센터를 절반으로 축소 (지역 반발 때문)  **에너지**: - **Helion**이 핵융합으로 **4억 6,500만 달러** 모금 (1.5억 °C 돌파) - Antares: 민간 로켓 최초 기록 - 퇴역한 Waymo 로보택시 배터리를 캘리포니아·텍사스 전력망에 에너지 저장장치로 재활용 - 미국 정부가 “깨끗하고 아름다운 석탄”에 **7억 달러** 투자 (초지능 붐을 뒷받침하기 위한 에너지 확보)  ---  ### 5. 생물학·의료 분야  **Caltech Sidewinder DNA 합성**: - 1천만 번 접합당 1번 오류 (매우 정확) - 대장균 게놈(12,500개 염기)을 며칠 만에 오류 없이 합성 가능  **AI 설계 백신**: - 케임브리지 대학에서 최초 시험 - 목표: 모든 코로나바이러스 + 독감 + 에볼라까지 예방  ---  ### 6. 정치·경제·법적 움직임  **Anthropic IPO 준비**: - 백악관과의 갈등 해소 중 - “공급망 위험” 꼬리표를 떼기 위해 노력  **캐나다**: - **5억 캐나다 달러** 규모 펀드 조성 - “모두를 위한 AI” 정책으로 **25만 개 일자리** 창출 + GDP 3% (약 2천억 캐나다 달러) 상승 목표  **아르헨티나 (Milei 대통령)**: - AI 에이전트에게 **법적 보호 장치** 부여 - “비인간 기업(Non-human company)” 개념 도입 → AI가 독립적인 판단으로 책임 제한을 받는 회사 형태 가능  **Brian Chesky (Airbnb CEO)**: - AI 연구소에 자금 지원 (상호작용·디자인 중심, Altman과 경쟁 구도)  **Pump.fun**: - 5달러 에스크로로 누구에게든 어떤 작업이든 지불할 수 있는 플랫폼 출시  ---  **한 줄 요약**:  2026년 6월 현재, **AI의 재귀적 자기 개선**이 실제 데이터로 확인되기 시작하면서, 에너지·생물학·소비자·정치 영역까지 AI가 전방위적으로 확장되고 있습니다. 특히 Anthropic의 내부 수치(문제 해결 능력 22%→64%)는 “AI가 AI를 가속화하는 시대”가 생각보다 빠르게 오고 있음을 보여주는 강력한 신호입니다.  ---  필요하시면 특정 부분(재귀적 자기 개선, 에너지, 생물학, 정치 움직임 등)을 더 깊게 정리해 드릴게요!

나의 친애하는 고졸들아,  오늘은 저번에 ‘시장에서 죽지 않는법’에 이어 ‘시장에서 장기간 꾸준히 돈버는법’ 알려준다.  대부분 고졸들이 들어 봤지만, 실천 하지 못하는거.  지수 사서 장기 보유하는 거다.  지수는 여러 가지가 있는데 기본은 s&p500을, 공격적인 지수는 나스닥100을 말한다.  “지수 사서 연 10퍼씩 먹고 언제 부자돼 ㅆㅂ” 라고 생각 하겠지.  나도 그랬다.  근데 이번에 봤지? 결국 괴롭고 힘들때 우리 계좌와 심리를 든든하게 잡아주는건 지수다.  말만 들으면 너무 뻔하고 지루하지?  내가 안쓰는 캐나다 계좌에 약 15년전 10주씩 남겨놓은 qqq랑 spy 수익률을 실제로 보면, 우리 지수의 경이로움을 볼수 있는데  현재 Qqq는 대충 16배 Spy는 대충 9배 정도 올라있다.  10억을 넣어놨다 하면 지금 Qqq는 160억 Spy는 90억이다.  그동안 내가 16배 수익을 냈는가? 9배라도 수익을 냈는가?  나도 이래저래 매매 타이밍 맞춘다고 나대고, 위아래로 발라 먹을 거라고 설치고 하면서 고뇌하며 까먹은 돈과 시간동안, 우리 지수는 묵묵히 복리로 수익률을 올려주고 있었던 것.  전에도 쓴적 있지만, 나도 본격적으로 의미있는 금액의 돈을 벌기 시작한 때는 장기 지수 투자를 메인으로 두고, 공격 계좌를 따로 운영하기 시작할때 부터이다.  날고 긴다는 월가의 90%의 액티브 펀드가 15년동안 에센피를 못 이겼다는 통계가 괜히 나온게 아니다.  나는 이 진리를 너무 늦게 깨달은 것이 심히 원통하다.   이 진리를 진심으로 일찍 깨우쳐 실행에 먼저 옮기는 고졸이, 서울 사이버 대학, 야간대를 넘어 꿈의 인서울 4년제의 대업을 달성할 수 있다.

sample_tweets.text: 미국 정부의 AI 기업 지분 인수 검토  $NVDA, $MSFT, $MRVL, $NBIS, $CRWV, $MU, $ORCL, $BE  도널드 트럼프 미국 대통령은 최근 미국 정부가 AI 기업들의 지분을 확보하여 이를 '미국 대중과의 파트너십' 형태로 운영하는 방안을 검토 중이라고 밝혔습니다.  배경: 최근 미국 정부 관계자들이 AI 기업들과 정부의 지분 매입 가능성에 대해 사전 논의를 진행했다는 보도가 나왔으며, 트럼프 대통령은 다음 주 중 AI 기업 경영진들과의 회의를 계획하고 있습니다.  정부의 AI 규제 행보: 트럼프 행정부는 AI 규제 방향을 두고 고심하고 있습니다. 지난 5월 말 예정되었던 AI 행정명령 서명식을 기술 업계의 반발로 취소했으나, 최근 수정된 행정명령에 서명했습니다. 이 명령은 AI 기업들이 신규 모델을 출시하기 전 정부의 사이버 보안 테스트를 자발적으로 제출하도록 하는 내용을 담고 있습니다.  우려 사항: 특히 앤스로픽(Anthropic)이 출시한 강력한 AI 도구인 '미토스(Mythos)'가 악용될 경우, 사이버 공격을 가속화하여 금융권 등 주요 기반 시설에 심각한 피해를 줄 수 있다는 우려가 커지고 있습니다.  정부의 기업 개입 사례: 이미 트럼프 행정부는 인텔(Intel)을 비롯해 희토류 및 양자 기술 관련 기업들의 지분을 확보하는 등 미국 기업 생태계에서 이례적으로 큰 역할을 수행해 온 바 있습니다.

The goal is not to replace DFIR tools.  The goal is to let outputs from existing tools and system reports bloom into one pressure topology:  PE-sieve → memory/process anomaly lane THOR → IOC/YARA/known-issue lane AIDA64 → host baseline lane UWC → resource-burden fingerprint lane Windows errors → cascade/origin lane Drivers / BIOS / UEFI → root-chain context  Then the engine asks:  What can this evidence honestly support? Where is the likely root-pressure candidate? What is observed? What is inferred? What is not proven? What cannot settle yet?

Jump Lists reveal what users actually do — not just what they claim.  Every time a user opens a document from Word, Excel, or any taskbar-pinned app, Windows records it in Jump Lists. These artifacts live in C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Recent\AutomaticDestinations (for recent items) and CustomDestinations (for pinned items).  Each Jump List file is named by Application ID (AppID) and contains full file paths, access timestamps, and interaction metadata. This makes them gold for incident response: you can prove which documents an insider accessed, trace lateral movement through RDP sessions, or identify data staging before exfiltration.  Parse them with JLECmd (Eric Zimmerman) or JumpListExplorer. AutomaticDestinations files are compound binary format; CustomDestinations are LNK-based. Both give you a timeline of user intent and file interaction that registry keys and prefetch can't match.  If you're investigating data theft, privilege abuse, or user activity timelines, Jump Lists should be in your first collection pass. They're small, persistent, and brutally honest about what users touched and when.  #DFIR #DigitalForensics

Troops Foil Kidnap Attempt in Plateau, Gunmen Flee  By: Zagazola Makama   Troops of Sector 3 of Operation Enduring Peace foiled an attempted kidnapping in Dong village, Jos North Local Government Area of Plateau State.  Security sources said the troops responded swiftly to a distress call at about 12:20 a.m. on June 7 following reports that unidentified gunmen had attempted to abduct a resident, Mr Moses Isa, from his home.  According to the sources, troops deployed under Operation Enduring Peace (OPEP) immediately mobilised to the village upon receiving the report.  On arrival at the scene, the troops established contact with the suspected kidnappers, leading to a brief exchange of fire.  “The armed men fled into the surrounding mountainous terrain due to the superior firepower and rapid response of the troops,” the sources said.  The sources added that troops expended eight rounds of 7.62mm special ammunition during the encounter.  No casualty was reported and the intended victim was successfully rescued from the attempted abduction.  Following the operation, troops dominated the general area and conducted aggressive patrols to prevent the suspects from regrouping or carrying out further criminal activities.  Security officials said efforts were ongoing to track and apprehend the fleeing suspects.  They urged residents to remain vigilant and continue providing timely information to security agencies to support ongoing operations against kidnapping and other criminal activities in the state.  The incident is the latest in a series of security operations aimed at curbing criminality and enhancing public safety across Plateau State.

KOGI STATE GOVERNMENT PRESS RELEASE  SECURITY OPERATIVES FOIL TERROR ATTACK IN IKOYI IJUMU, BANDITS SUFFER CASUALTIES  1. The Kogi State Government has confirmed that security operatives successfully foiled a terrorist attack in Ikoyi, Ijumu Local Government Area, preventing what could have resulted in a major security crisis in the State. The incident occurred at about 7:00 p.m. on Saturday, June 6, 2026, when a group of Tiv residents violated the State Government's security protocol restricting public social gatherings beyond 4:00 p.m. Taking advantage of the gathering, armed bandits launched an attack with the apparent intention of abducting participants and creating panic within the community.  2. However, in a swift and coordinated response that demonstrated the effectiveness of Kogi State's security architecture, personnel of the Nigerian Military, officers of the Nigeria Police Force, operatives of the Kogi State Vigilante Service, and local hunters mobilized to the scene within five minutes of the attack. The "Israeli Espionage" Affair in the US: Not Just Intelligence - A Battle Over the Boundaries of Influence  Eldad Shavit, a senior researcher at INSS, writes:  The recent publications in the US regarding suspicion of Israeli intelligence activity do not stand on their own. They come at a particularly sensitive timing, in which Washington is trying to advance contacts with Iran and to calm the Lebanese arena, while Israel fears that the emerging arrangements will limit its freedom of action, leave dangerous strategic assets in Iran's hands, or force understandings upon it that do not align with its perception of the threat. Therefore, the main question is not only whether Israel did indeed try to gather information on internal discussions in the US, but why American officials chose to surface the claim precisely now.  According to a report in the New York Times, Israel was largely sidelined from the American contacts with Iran, and learned about them through regional diplomatic sources and also via intelligence gathering, including surveillance of senior American officials. Concurrently, NBC reported that the Pentagon has raised the risk/espionage threat assessment by Israel to the highest level in recent weeks. Israel denied this, and the White House also sought to downplay the significance of things, but the publication itself has already created a political problem.  The timing teaches that this is more than an accidental leak. It is possible that elements in the Pentagon and the intelligence community wish to warn against over-exposure of sensitive information to Israel, especially when there is concern that Jerusalem will act to derail American moves that it does not favor. It is also possible that this signals to elements in the administration: closeness to Trump does not grant Israel unrestricted access to briefing rooms in Washington, and certainly does not grant it veto power over American policy toward Iran.  It is also important to note the potential gap between the White House and the professional defense establishment. Even if the political echelon around Trump wishes to preserve the closeness to Israel and downplay the significance of the publications, the Pentagon and the intelligence community operate according to a different logic: safeguarding the independence of the decision-making process, protecting sensitive information, and restricting the access of foreign actors, even when dealing with a close ally.  In this sense, the publication also serves an internal American purpose. It warns senior officials in the administration, Congress, and the defense establishment against uncontrolled sharing of information with Israelis, and signals that the dispute with Jerusalem is not just over policy but also over trust. As long as Israel is perceived as trying to bypass the official channels, the tendency in Washington will strengthen to tighten procedures, reduce exposure, and manage sensitive contacts in more closed circles. Furthermore, the publication provides ammunition to those seeking to frame Israel not just as a demanding ally, but as an actor operating leverage within Washington to narrow the administration's maneuver space.

Taiwan is adopting Israel as a model in preparing its society, economy, and civilian front for the growing security threat posed by China.  “Beijing uses every possible tool to isolate Taiwan. We are their number one target,” Deputy Foreign Minister Chen Ming-chi toldYnet News in a recent interview. “In the past year alone, we identified 45,000 official Chinese accounts that spread some three million false posts against Taiwan.”  Taiwan produces around 80% of the world’s semiconductors, one of the most crucial and strategic technology components of the 21st century, making a potential military attack by China a global economic and strategic emergency.  For Taiwan, Israel is a “point of deep identification and a source of operational lessons,” according to the Ynet report, which is home to 23 million people. Taiwanese officials say they relate to Israel’s position as a small democracy facing persistent security threats, and they also express admiration for what they describe as the resilience and determination of Israeli society across multiple sectors.  “The military and diplomatic support you receive from the United States and the Western world is far beyond what Taiwan receives,” said a representative of the Straits Exchange Foundation, the body responsible for economic relations with China. “Israel does excellent work in gathering intelligence and fighting the infiltration of spies. That is something we must improve. But beyond that, we see the determination of your people. We need to learn from you how to put disagreements aside.”  Last month, a delegation of Israeli lawmakers visited Taiwan, where they met with President Lai Ching-te as part of an effort to strengthen ties in technology, trade, security, resilience and academic cooperation. The delegation included Knesset Members Mickey Levy, Boaz Toporovsky and Ron Katz from the Yesh Atid party and Yonatan Mishraki from the Shas party.  Levy said the purpose of the visit was “to demonstrate Israel’s close ties with Taiwan, noting that Taiwan was among the first countries to express support for Israel following the Oct. 7, 2023, attack by Hamas.” He also expressed hope of further advancing bilateral cooperation in economic, security and technology.  Meanwhile, daily life in Taiwan appears largely routine on the surface, but extensive preparations for a potential Chinese invasion are underway. More than 100,000 citizens have reportedly already been trained for such scenarios by Kuma Academy, a civil defense organization.  “About 90% of Taiwan’s population does not serve actively in the military,” said Kuma Academy CEO Fuming Chu. “Our role is to prepare them for emergencies without making them paranoid. We teach first aid, rescue, logistics and, critically, how to identify disinformation.”

Not a fodder for politics: Orire abduction & security challenges  By Tunde Rahman  The recent abduction of schoolchildren in Ahoro-Esiele Community, Orire Local Government Area of Oyo State, remains deeply distressing. On May 15, 2026, heartless kidnappers abducted 38 schoolchildren, killing one teacher in a heinous assault on our nation’s security and educational system.   The Nigerian Union of Teachers and students across the country have taken to the streets in protest. The action is understandable.  However, this and similar tragic incidents should never be used as political fodder, especially in the midst of our country’s challenging security situation.  Anyone who has fallen victim to kidnapping or is a close relation of a victim will appreciate the enormity of the pain and trauma involved. I know how it feels because I went through such trauma in April when my cook, who had a phobia of air travel, was kidnapped in broad daylight in Kabba, Kogi State, while travelling from Lagos to Abuja. He was one of the 18 passengers in a Chisco Transport Company bus abducted at gunpoint alongside their driver by daredevil bandits on April 15. He spent 13 days in captivity before we were able to secure his release through a combined effort coordinated by the Commissioner of Police in Kogi State. The ordeal he described—at the hands of kidnappers, mostly Fulani, with only one able to speak even basic English—was harrowing. What the families of the 18 abducted endured to secure their release is better left unsaid.  With more cases of kidnapping making the headlines, there is a tendency for some to think the government is helpless and not doing enough to combat this scourge. This perception is simply inaccurate.   President Bola Tinubu views the security situation as an existential threat. He is doing everything possible, leaving no stone unturned to put the security monster in a chokehold and ensure that the security and safety of Nigerians – the raison d’étre of government – is guaranteed.  After the Orire abduction, the President sent a high-powered delegation led by his Chief of Staff, Hon. Femi Gbajabiamila, including the National Security Adviser, Nuhu Ribadu and Minister of Defence, General Christopher Musa, among others, to empathise with the families and relations of the victims and comfort the leaders and people of Ogbomoso and Orire communities. The visit was in addition to the ongoing efforts by security agents to trace and apprehend the kidnappers,  secure the release of the abducted students and teachers, bring them back to their families and also bring the criminals to justice.   There are indications that the government is close to securing the safe release of the abducted children and teachers. The government has not abdicated its responsibility and continues to work tirelessly to strengthen security across the country. To those sponsoring or exploiting kidnapping and banditry for political gain ahead of 2027, your days are numbered—the government will find you and bring you to justice.  This new security challenge presents a significant opportunity to restate what the Tinubu administration has been doing to confront insecurity in the land, protect Nigerian citizens, and end the security problem for good. These efforts have already yielded some positive results. The desired full outcome will soon emerge.

Well, we finally figured this out. My mom lives in a quadraplex apartment and they've been renovating the building as people moved out these last few years and every time they renovated an apartment they consolidated the power onto her unit, hence it basically quadrupling over the years.  We found out because my sister walked around trying to find the meters and could only find one and then talking to the neighbors and finding out electric was included in their rent. My sister is taking point on talking to the landlord for now to get reimbursed but since that's probably going nowhere it looks like we're getting the utility company and a lawyer involved about theft of services.

I have a quite embarrassing story about being brought in on a weeks long issue, assuming that basic troubleshooting had been done, going down all kinds of rabbit holes, only to reset myself, compare to a known working system, and find that they were pointed at a test environment.

I share this blog with anyone starting out with ASR, as this is the proper way to do it.  I either see environments where everything just stays in Audit Mode and others where they immediately set everything in Block Mode and then we have a mess to comb through.

I think it's a bit of a problem in the spec market of the ten-page rule. It's writing for the reader rather than the movie. The reader doesn't have time to waste on hundreds of bad horror scripts, so they want a promise of premise, threat, and a snapshot of the world and iconography in the first ten, which makes sense for them, but doesn't really make for a good story where you care and are invested. Not to say ten pages without a cold open can't be gripping and well written. Of course, they can, but what gripped me in a theater in the first few scenes of Obsession might not have gripped me in the first ten pages of a read.
If you would have told me in 2016 that computers would be the ones falling for NPM typosquatting in 2026 I would have called you an idiot.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협 환경은 생성형 AI를 활용한 공격의 정교화와 자동화가 핵심 트렌드로 자리 잡았습니다. 특히 AI Agent를 악용한 자동화된 취약점 탐색 및 공격 수행(AI Worm)이 현실화되고 있으며, 기존의 보안 프레임워크인 MITRE ATT&CK이 AI 기반 공격 패턴을 충분히 포괄하지 못한다는 우려가 제기되고 있습니다. 또한, SolarWinds Serv-U와 같은 인프라 소프트웨어의 취약점이 활발히 악용되고 있으며, 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack) 및 WordPress 플러그인 취약점을 통한 대규모 사이버 범죄가 지속적으로 보고되고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **RipperSec-II & TheComrades Group** (CyberAsia_io): 이스라엘 에너지 기업(Delek Group) 및 요식업 웹사이트를 타겟으로 한 친팔레스타인 성향의 핵티비즘 활동.
- **Black X** (AhnLab ASEC): 대한민국 성형외과 대상 내부 데이터 유출 및 랜섬웨어 공격 수행.
- **Qilin** (AhnLab ASEC): 대한민국 자동화 설비 기업을 대상으로 한 랜섬웨어 공격 및 데이터 강탈.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **BGF네트웍스(CU POST)**: 외부 해킹 공격으로 인한 고객 개인정보 유출 사고 발생.
- **한국퀀텀컴퓨팅(KQC) & LS ITC**: 양자내성암호(PQC) 기반 보안 기술 검증 완료(Agent AI 및 양자 컴퓨터 위협 대응 목적).
- **AhnLab ASEC 보고**: Qilin 랜섬웨어 그룹의 국내 자동화 설비 기업 공격 및 Black X 그룹의 성형외과 데이터 유출 확인.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
1. **CVE-2026-3300**: Everest Forms Pro (WordPress), 심각도 Critical, 활발히 악용 중.
2. **CVE-2026-45695**: Kopia backup server, 심각도 Critical, 인증되지 않은 RCE 취약점.
3. **CVE-2026-9082**: Drupal, 심각도 Critical, 활발히 악용 중인 SQL Injection 취약점.
4. **CVE-2026-0257**: PAN-OS, 심각도 High, 활발히 악용 중.
5. **CVE-2026-28318**: SolarWinds Serv-U, 심각도 High, CISA KEV 목록 등재 및 활발히 악용 중.

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**:
    - CVE-2026-3300 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/critical-everest-forms-pro-flaw-exploited-to-take-over-wordpress-sites/))
    - CVE-2026-0257 ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/active-exploitation-of-pan-os-cve-2026-0257/))
    - CVE-2026-21404 ([CISA ICS Advisories](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/ics-advisories/icsa-26-155-01))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **패치 적용**: SolarWinds Serv-U(15.5.4 HF1 이상), Everest Forms Pro, PAN-OS 등 KEV 목록에 포함된 취약점의 최신 보안 패치를 즉시 적용하십시오.
- **AI 보안 정책 수립**: 기업 내 AI Agent 도입 시 데이터 유출 방지를 위해 ChatGPT Lockdown Mode와 같은 보안 기능을 활성화하고, AI 워크플로우에 대한 접근 제어를 강화하십시오.
- **공급망 및 의존성 점검**: OWASP CVE Lite CLI 등을 활용하여 프로젝트 내 취약한 의존성 패키지를 식별하고, 비정상적인 외부 스크립트(Polyfill 등) 삽입 여부를 모니터링하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8/10
- **근거**: AI 기반의 자동화된 공격이 실전 배치되고 있으며, 국내외 주요 인프라 및 기업을 대상으로 한 랜섬웨어와 데이터 유출 사고가 동시다발적으로 발생하고 있어 위협 수준이 매우 높음.
